_comment: Single source of truth. PASSED=proven edge. RESEARCH=paper trading only. FAILED=edge not present.

H001.title: Liquidity sweep + HTF trend alignment
H001.lesson: Unfiltered sweeps have no directional edge.

H002.title: Qualified liquidity sweep (ATR>=0.5 + TRENDING regime) — EURUSD only
H002.tested_on: EURUSD Yahoo H1 2yr, n=76, wr=0.5789, p=0.2209
H002.lesson: EURUSD appeared promising at n=76 but was false positive.

H002b.title: Qualified sweep — multi-symbol aggregation (EURUSD+GBPUSD+XAUUSD)
H002b.tested_on: 3 symbols, n=232, combined wr=0.4612 (-3.66pp vs H001), p=0.4337
H002b.lesson: EURUSD WR=57.89% was false positive. At n=232 across 3 symbols, combined WR=46.12% is WORSE than H001 baseline. ATR+regime filter does NOT improve sweep win rate. Do not pursue this direction further.
H002b.conclusion: ABANDON — liquidity sweep as entry signal is not a proven edge on any of the 3 tested symbols. The SMC sweep concept needs a fundamentally different formulation (e.g. BOS+FVG combination, not standalone sweep).

H003.title: ICT killzone + premium/discount zone bias
H003.notes: Paper trading. Data collecting via engine_tracker.

H004.title: NNFX EMA200 baseline + ADX strength filter

H006.title: Wyckoff Spring/Upthrust detection
H006.notes: Paper trading. Price-only for FX, volume for metals.

H008.title: BOS (Break of Structure) + FVG (Fair Value Gap) confluence entry
H008.notes: Next hypothesis after H001+H002+H002b all failed on sweep-based entries. BOS+FVG is structurally different — requires price to FIRST break structure (confirm direction) THEN fill an imbalance (FVG) before entry. Design experiment before running.
H008.rationale: H001/H002/H002b tested: enter ON the sweep. H008 tests: enter AFTER BOS confirms the sweep was real, at FVG retracement. This adds two confirmation layers that sweep-only entries lack.
